nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

nodes.name: 美國南部
nodes.summary: 美國南部是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联6种鸟类，包括绿头鸭、美洲蛇鹈、大藍鷺等6种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 雁形目
nodes.summary: 雁形目（Anseriformes）是图谱中的目级分类节点，当前关联166种鸟类，如中华秋沙鸭、角叫鴨、黑颈冠叫鸭等166种鸟类。

nodes.name: 衛報
nodes.summary: 衛報是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联2种鸟类，包括绿头鸭、鵰鴞，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: Northern Mexico
nodes.summary: Northern Mexico是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括绿头鸭，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 中美洲
nodes.summary: 中美洲是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联125种鸟类，包括大䳍、小穴䳍、灰胸穴䳍等125种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 河口
nodes.summary: 淡水与海水交汇形成的高生产力边缘生态系统。

nodes.name: 猎捕压力
nodes.summary: 直接猎捕或误捕造成额外死亡。

nodes.name: 鸭科
nodes.summary: 鸭科（Anatidae）是图谱中的科级分类节点，当前关联162种鸟类，如中华秋沙鸭、白背鸭、细斑树鸭等162种鸟类。

nodes.name: 加勒比地区
nodes.summary: 加勒比地区是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联53种鸟类，包括小穴䳍、黑嘴樹鴨、桂紅鴨等53种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 绿头鸭
nodes.summary: 绿头鸭（Mallard，学名 Anas platyrhynchos），分类上属于雁形目，并归入鸭科，在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联7个分布地点节点，常见栖息地包括湿地、海岸湿地、河口，主要威胁包括猎捕压力。

nodes.name: 海岸湿地
nodes.summary: 海湾、泻湖、盐沼与河口组成的复合湿地。

nodes.name: 紐埃
nodes.summary: 紐埃是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联4种鸟类，包括绿头鸭、Crimson-crowned fruit dove、钩嘴圆尾鹱等4种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 新西蘭國家廣播電台
nodes.summary: 新西蘭國家廣播電台是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括绿头鸭，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 湿地
nodes.summary: 浅水、沼泽与植被斑块组成的高生产力生态系统。

Also in the east, it ranges to south-eastern and south-western Australia and New Zealand in the Southern Hemisphere. It is strongly migratory in the northern parts of its breeding range, and winters farther south. For example, in North America, it winters south to the southern United States and northern Mexico,{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=ILrZ4fhHnGcC|title=Final environmental statement for the issuance of annual regulations permitting the sport hunting of migratory birds|last=Service|first=U

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: Belonging to the subfamily Anatinae of the waterfowl family Anatidae, mallards live in wetlands, eat water plants and small animals, and are social animals preferring to congregate in groups or flocks of varying sizes

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: Dale Guthrie|last=Guthrie|first=Russell Dale|date=2001|publisher=Archaeopress|isbn=978-1-84171-236-9|language=en}} It is found in both fresh- and salt-water wetlands, including parks, small ponds, rivers, lakes and estuaries, as well as shallow inlets and open sea within sight of the coastline. Water depths of less than are preferred, with birds avoiding areas more than a few metres deep. They are attracted to bodies of water with aquatic vegetation

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: Dale Guthrie|last=Guthrie|first=Russell Dale|date=2001|publisher=Archaeopress|isbn=978-1-84171-236-9|language=en}} It is found in both fresh- and salt-water wetlands, including parks, small ponds, rivers, lakes and estuaries, as well as shallow inlets and open sea within sight of the coastline. Water depths of less than are preferred, with birds avoiding areas more than a few metres deep. They are attracted to bodies of water with aquatic vegetation

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: Ecological changes and hunting have also led to a decline of local species; for example, the New Zealand grey duck population declined drastically due to overhunting in the mid-20th century. Hybrid offspring of Hawaiian ducks seem to be less well adapted to native habitat, and using them in re-introduction projects apparently reduces success. In summary, the problems of mallards "hybridising away" relatives is more a consequence of local ducks declining than of mallards spreading; allopatric speciation and isolating behaviour have produced today's diversity of mallard-like ducks despite the fact that, in most, if not all, of these populations, hybridisation must have occurred to some extent.{{Cite journal |last1=Tubaro |first1=Pablo L
